text: US President Trump, all political theatre, has just discovered the refining (and marketing) margin. https://t.co/4u1q5Xz5qm

“… Physical crude oil cargoes are selling at discounts across the globe, changing trade flows as markets come under pressure from fast-rising Middle Eastern supply …”

https://t.co/66ewWzq4v6

China’s oil buying strike has sent the value of physical crude from Angola to a >10-year low vs the Dated Brent benchmark. Beijing is typically a big importer of Angolan crude, but Chinese companies are now sellers, rather than buyers.

Brent crude drops below $75 a barrel for the first time since the start of the U.S.-Iran war. 

📉⤵️ is Brent year-to-date (red line is Feb 27, 2026, the day before the conflict broke out) https://t.co/z6zkoIBm6k
Brent crude oil prompt time spread flips into contango.
Is every day WTI crude oil stays above $65 still a great day?
Key parts of the oil market are suddenly awash in supply, as a stream of cargoes out of the Strait of Hormuz accelerates after the US-Iran agreement to open the waterway. https://t.co/wtgG5GtvwF
